refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data acquisition, processing, and dissemination meet latency targets.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition, processing, and dissemination, each critical to timely worksite model updates.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data capture to worksite model update availability under representative operational scenarios; verify p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.
sub_frs: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency budget
Process and integrate telemetry data to update the worksite model within latency constraints
Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency targets

candidate_dps: High-throughput telemetry data pipeline with prioritized message queues
Edge computing nodes on vehicles for local pre-processing
Centralized worksite model update service with real-time synchronization
Optimized network protocols with QoS for telemetry and model dissemination
selected_dp.selected_dp: High-throughput telemetry data pipeline with prioritized message queues combined with centralized worksite model update service with real-time synchronization

potential_sources: Telemetry data acquisition tightly coupled with processing service
Network link dependency for data dissemination
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but designed with clear interface boundaries to minimize ripple effects.

assumptions_risks: Network link latency and reliability meet minimum QoS requirements
Telemetry data formats are standardized and stable
Processing algorithms can meet latency targets under peak load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and consider fallback mechanisms if latency targets are not met.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update dissemination

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between NGHE Solution and Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model and tasking information to Teleoperation module

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends safety-related updates to Safety Services module

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry processing and model update logic, minimizing diffusion.
Primarily data source; limited processing to reduce latency.
Consumes updated model; no processing responsibility.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update logic in NGHE Solution module reduces latency and diffusion, improving maintainability and verification.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module is overloaded
Dependency on network link reliability for timely data flow
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider load balancing or distributed processing if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement clearly decomposes latency requirement into measurable sub-functions; selected DP logically addresses these with feasible solutions.

diffusion_risks: Centralized processing may create single point of failure or performance bottleneck
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: IM assignment to NGHE Solution module aligns well with DP, minimizing diffusion and clarifying ownership.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end on NGHE Solution module using telemetry and network link interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities and interfaces supports traceability and verification.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance and consider fallback or redundancy
Evaluate potential for distributed processing if latency targets are challenged

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet requirements and constraints with clear ownership and verification paths; no immediate need to revise or zigzag.
next_level_sub_frs: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency budget
Process and integrate telemetry data to update the worksite model within latency constraints
Disseminate updated worksite model to relevant modules and stakeholders within latency targets

revise.why: No deficiencies identified requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Processing algorithms perform as expected under load
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under representative operational scenarios
Load testing results of processing service
